+# Execution Report
+
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-367
+AUTHOR: Executor / Codex
+TIMESTAMP: 2026-06-10_09-12-25+0300
+RELATED_FILES:
+- crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs - added viewport-gated mouse-wheel camera zoom routing.
+- crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/tests.rs - added focused lifecycle tests for wheel delta signs, viewport gating, no-op paths, and camera invariants.
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-367_EXEC_2026-06-10_09-12-25+0300.md - execution report.
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-367_EXEC_2026-06-10_09-12-25+0300.meta.json - sidecar expected from finalization.
+STATUS: AWAITING_REVIEW
+
+## Task Packet Reference
+
+`ai_handoffs/ISSUE-367_TASK_2026-06-10_08-48-14+0300.md`
+
+## Pre-Execution Review Consumed
+
+No pre-execution review issued for this dispatch.
+
+## Current-State Claims: Falsification Re-run
+
+- Claim: `WindowEvent::MouseWheel` was not currently routed in editor-shell lifecycle source or tests.
+  Re-run before edits: `git grep -n "MouseWheel" -- crates/editor-shell/src crates/editor-shell/tests` -> no matches; command exited 1.
+- Claim: editor-shell currently had no `MouseScrollDelta`, `LineDelta`, or `PixelDelta` handling in source or tests.
+  Re-run before edits: `git grep -n "MouseScrollDelta\|LineDelta\|PixelDelta" -- crates/editor-shell/src crates/editor-shell/tests` -> no matches; command exited 1.
+- Claim: optional `viewport_navigation.rs` helper module was not tracked.
+  Re-run: `git ls-files -- 'crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/viewport_navigation.rs'` -> no output.
+- Claim: `EditorShell::is_pointer_over_viewport_tab` returns false before host delegation when there is no egui host or no cursor position.
+  Re-run: `git grep -n -A16 -B4 "pub(crate) fn is_pointer_over_viewport_tab" -- crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs` -> confirmed the `egui_host.as_ref()` and `cursor_pos` guards return `false` before calling `host.is_pointer_over_viewport(cursor)`.
+- Claim: host viewport hit-test returns false when there is no captured viewport rect.
+  Re-run: `git grep -n -A14 -B2 "pub fn is_pointer_over_viewport" -- crates/editor-egui-host/src/lib.rs` -> confirmed `let Some(rect) = self.viewport_tab_rect() else { return false; };` plus defensive pixel-scale false guards before `rect.contains(logical)`.
+- Claim: lifecycle tests already covered no-host and no-cursor viewport hit-test behavior.
+  Re-run: `git grep -n -A14 -B4 "fresh_shell_reports_pointer_not_over_viewport\|shell_with_no_cursor_pos_reports_pointer_not_over_viewport" -- crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/tests.rs` -> confirmed both tests assert `!shell.is_pointer_over_viewport_tab()`.
+- Claim: lifecycle tests already covered zoom target, up vector, FOV, near/far clip, distance, and normalized direction preservation for existing zoom helpers.
+  Re-run: `git grep -n -A50 -B6 "fn zoom_camera_in_and_out_preserve_target_and_direction" -- crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/tests.rs` -> confirmed target/up/FOV/near/far/direction assertions around `zoom_camera_in` and `zoom_camera_out`.
+- Claim: existing left-click route demonstrates viewport-tab fallthrough using `is_pointer_over_viewport_tab`.
+  Re-run: `git grep -n -A36 -B4 "WindowEvent::MouseInput { state, button" -- crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs` -> confirmed the branch computes `over_viewport = self.is_pointer_over_viewport_tab()` and passes it to `should_fire_face_pick`.
+
+## What I Changed
+
+### Source
+
+- `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs:2057`: added private `zoom_camera_for_viewport_mouse_wheel`, which reuses `zoom_camera_in` for positive vertical deltas and `zoom_camera_out` for negative vertical deltas.
+- `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs:2396`: added private `viewport_mouse_wheel_zoom_direction` and `ViewportMouseWheelZoom` for line/pixel vertical sign handling. Horizontal-only or zero vertical delta returns no action.
+- `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs:2576`: added a `WindowEvent::MouseWheel` arm that computes `self.is_pointer_over_viewport_tab()` and applies wheel zoom only through the viewport-gated helper.
+
+### Tests
+
+- `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/tests.rs::viewport_mouse_wheel_signs_map_line_and_pixel_vertical_delta`: pins line and pixel positive/negative/zero vertical delta mapping and the false viewport-gate no-op.
+- `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/tests.rs::viewport_mouse_wheel_positive_vertical_delta_zooms_in_preserving_invariants`: verifies positive wheel input over the viewport reduces a 10.0 eye-target distance to 8.0 and preserves target, up, FOV, near/far, and view direction.
+- `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/tests.rs::viewport_mouse_wheel_negative_vertical_delta_zooms_out_preserving_invariants`: verifies negative pixel wheel input over the viewport increases a 10.0 eye-target distance to 12.5 and preserves the same camera invariants.
+- `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/tests.rs::viewport_mouse_wheel_horizontal_only_delta_is_no_op`: verifies horizontal-only line and pixel wheel movement does not move the camera.
+- `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/tests.rs::viewport_mouse_wheel_false_viewport_hit_test_is_no_op`: verifies wheel input does not zoom when the viewport hit-test result is false.
+- `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/tests.rs::viewport_mouse_wheel_no_cursor_or_no_host_is_no_op`: verifies the shell-side hit-test false path leaves the camera unchanged when there is no cursor or no host.
+
+### Docs
+
+- No task bookkeeping docs were changed; `Status.md`, `HANDOFF.md`, and `change.md` did not need updates for this scoped code change.
+
+## Per-File Summary
+
+- `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs`: only editor-shell lifecycle routing changed. No commands, accelerators, menus, route replacement, CAD, clipboard, undo, or dirty-state behavior was added.
+- `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/tests.rs`: added pure/helper-level lifecycle coverage to avoid requiring a real winit event loop, GPU surface, or egui-host edit.
+
+## Verification Results
+
+- `cargo test -p rge-editor-shell --lib` -> exit 1 in this sandbox before compile because `CARGO_TARGET_DIR` is set to `A:\RustCache\target` and Cargo cannot open `A:\RustCache\target\debug\.cargo-lock` (`Access is denied. (os error 5)`).
+- `$env:CARGO_TARGET_DIR='target\ISSUE-367'; cargo test -p rge-editor-shell --lib` -> exit 0; 265 passed, 0 failed, 1 ignored.
+- `cargo check -p rge-editor-shell --lib` -> exit 1 in this sandbox for the same inaccessible `A:\RustCache\target\debug\.cargo-lock`.
+- `$env:CARGO_TARGET_DIR='target\ISSUE-367'; cargo check -p rge-editor-shell --lib` -> exit 0.
+- `cargo fmt --check` -> exit 0. It emitted repeated warnings that stable rustfmt cannot apply nightly-only `imports_granularity` and `group_imports` config options.
+- `git diff --check` -> exit 0.
+- `git diff --name-only` -> exit 0; tracked diff limited to `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs` and `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/tests.rs`.
+
+## Deviations from Task Packet
+
+None in implementation scope. The exact default-target Cargo commands could not complete in this sandbox because the inherited `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=A:\RustCache\target` is not writable/readable here; equivalent reruns with a workspace-local target directory passed.
+
+## Open Questions for Reviewer
+
+- Please note the Cargo target-dir environment issue above when re-running verification. The code/test gates pass when Cargo is allowed to use a writable target directory.
+- The no captured viewport-rect condition is covered through the same shell-side `over_viewport_tab == false` branch used by the host guard. I did not add an egui-host constructor/test because that would require editing a forbidden crate or constructing real wgpu/window primitives.
+
+## Worktree State
+
+- Tracked files: modified but uncommitted:
+  - `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs`
+  - `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/tests.rs`
+- Untracked items:
+  - `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-367_EXEC_2026-06-10_09-12-25+0300.md`
+  - `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-367_EXEC_2026-06-10_09-12-25+0300.meta.json` after finalization
+  - `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-367_TASK_2026-06-10_08-48-14+0300.md` (pre-existing task packet)
+  - `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-367_TASK_2026-06-10_08-48-14+0300.meta.json` (pre-existing task sidecar)
+  - `ai_handoffs/claims/ISSUE-367_2026-06-10_08-48-12-7479243+0300_claim.json` (pre-existing claim artifact)
+  - ignored `target/` build output from the workspace-local Cargo target override
+- Branch: `ai-dispatch/ISSUE-367`
+- Last commit: `fc57ec1 ai-dispatch ISSUE-364: Audit next Phase 9 editor-usability task (#365)`
+
+---
+
+HANDOFF_STATUS: COMPLETE
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-367
+AUTHOR: Executor / Codex
+NEXT_ROLE: REVIEWER_AI
+EXIT_CODE: 0
+
+---

+# Task Packet
+
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-367
+AUTHOR: Planner / OpenAI Codex
+TIMESTAMP: 2026-06-10_08-48-14+0300
+RELATED_FILES:
+- crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs
+- crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/tests.rs
+- crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/viewport_navigation.rs
+- Status.md
+- HANDOFF.md
+- change.md
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-367_EXEC_*.md
+- ai_dispatch_logs/log_*.md
+STATUS: OPEN
+
+## Goal
+
+Implement GitHub issue #367: route `WindowEvent::MouseWheel` inside `editor-shell` so mouse-wheel scroll over the Viewport tab zooms the editor camera using the existing `EditorShell::zoom_camera_in` / `EditorShell::zoom_camera_out` semantics. The change must be viewport-only, must preserve the current camera target and view direction invariants, and must not introduce any new command, accelerator, menu, CAD, clipboard, undo, dirty-state, or route-replacement behavior.
+
+## Scope
+
+### MAY edit
+- `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs`
+- `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/tests.rs`
+- `Status.md`
+- `HANDOFF.md`
+- `change.md`
+
+### MUST NOT edit
+- `crates/editor-egui-host/**`
+- `crates/editor-ui/**`
+- `crates/editor-actions/**`
+- `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/accelerator.rs`
+- `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/commands.rs`
+- `crates/cad-core/**`
+- `crates/cad-native/**`
+- `crates/cad-occt/**`
+- `crates/cad-projection/**`
+- `kernel/**`
+- `runtime/**`
+- `editor/rge-editor/**`
+- `Cargo.toml`
+- `Cargo.lock`
+- `**/Cargo.toml`
+- `.github/workflows/**`
+- `.ai/**`
+- `*.ps1`
+- `crates/plugin-discovery/**`
+- `crates/runtime-wasmtime/**`
+- `crates/runtime-wasmtime-engine/**`
+- `kernel/plugin-host/**`
+- Any source, test, docs, schema, script, workflow, automation, CAD/kernel/runtime, plugin-runtime, or handoff file not explicitly listed in MAY edit or MAY add new files.
+
+### MAY add new files
+- `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/viewport_navigation.rs`
+- `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-367_EXEC_*.md`
+- `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-367_EXEC_*.meta.json`
+- `ai_dispatch_logs/log_*.md`
+
+### MUST NOT add new files
+- New crates, Cargo manifests, Cargo lockfiles, workflows, schemas, scripts, automation files, or plugin-runtime files.
+- New source files other than `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/viewport_navigation.rs`.
+- New docs other than the existing task-bookkeeping files listed in MAY edit.
+- New TASK, REVIEW, CORRECT, or CLOSEOUT packets for this dispatch.
+- New commands, accelerators, menu definitions, route-replacement artifacts, CAD fixtures, clipboard fixtures, undo/dirty fixtures, or navigation systems beyond viewport mouse-wheel zoom.
+
+### Advisory machine-readable scope envelope
+
+<!-- handoff:envelope v1 -->
+MAY_EDIT:
+  - crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs
+  - crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/tests.rs

+  - change.md

+<!-- /handoff:envelope -->
+
+## Deliverables
+
+- A focused `WindowEvent::MouseWheel` route in `EditorShell::window_event` that checks whether the current pointer is over the Viewport tab body before changing the camera.
+- Reuse of the existing zoom helpers: vertical wheel delta greater than zero zooms in via `zoom_camera_in`, vertical wheel delta less than zero zooms out via `zoom_camera_out`, and zero vertical delta is a no-op. Horizontal wheel delta must not drive zoom by itself.
+- A small private helper is allowed, either inside `lifecycle/mod.rs` or in the optional `lifecycle/viewport_navigation.rs`, if it keeps delta/sign/gating tests simple and does not expose new public API.
+- Focused lifecycle tests covering delta direction, viewport-only gating, no-cursor/no-viewport no-op behavior, and preservation of the existing target/direction zoom invariants.
+- Updated task bookkeeping only if needed in `Status.md`, `HANDOFF.md`, and/or `change.md`.
+- One execution report packet under `ai_handoffs/` for this dispatch.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- `WindowEvent::MouseWheel` over the Viewport tab zooms the editor camera and uses the same eye-target distance changes as the existing View menu zoom helpers.
+- Wheel events outside the Viewport tab do not zoom, even if they are otherwise unconsumed by egui.
+- A shell with no egui host, no cursor position, or no captured viewport rect treats wheel input as a no-op and leaves the camera unchanged.
+- Positive vertical wheel delta routes to zoom-in behavior; negative vertical wheel delta routes to zoom-out behavior; zero vertical delta routes to no-op behavior.
+- The target, up vector, FOV, near/far clip planes, and normalized eye-target direction remain preserved according to the existing zoom invariant tests.
+- No command enum, command bus, accelerator, menu item, route-replacement, orbit, pan, drag, clipboard, CAD, undo-stack, or dirty-state behavior is added or changed.
+- No Cargo metadata or dependency file changes occur.
+- No forbidden paths from MUST NOT edit are modified.
+
+## Current-State Claims: Falsification Required
+
+Every negative / "unchanged" assertion this packet makes about current source MUST list its inverse-search command and result. A negative claim without a falsifying search is not a valid premise under Protocol Rule 8.
+
+- Claim: `WindowEvent::MouseWheel` is not currently routed in the editor-shell lifecycle source or tests.
+  Falsifying search: `git grep -n "MouseWheel" -- crates/editor-shell/src crates/editor-shell/tests` -> no matches; command exited 1.
+- Claim: editor-shell currently has no `MouseScrollDelta`, `LineDelta`, or `PixelDelta` handling in source or tests.
+  Falsifying search: `git grep -n "MouseScrollDelta\|LineDelta\|PixelDelta" -- crates/editor-shell/src crates/editor-shell/tests` -> no matches; command exited 1.
+- Claim: the optional `viewport_navigation.rs` helper module is not currently tracked.
+  Falsifying search: `git ls-files -- 'crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/viewport_navigation.rs'` -> no output.
+- Claim: `EditorShell::is_pointer_over_viewport_tab` currently returns `false` before host delegation when there is no egui host or no cursor position.
+  Falsifying read: `git grep -n -A12 -B2 "pub(crate) fn is_pointer_over_viewport_tab" -- crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs` -> lines 2158-2165 show `let Some(host) = self.egui_host.as_ref() else { return false; };`, `let Some(cursor) = self.cursor_pos else { return false; };`, then `host.is_pointer_over_viewport(cursor)`.
+- Claim: the host viewport hit-test currently returns `false` when there is no captured viewport rect.
+  Falsifying read: `git grep -n -A14 -B2 "pub fn is_pointer_over_viewport" -- crates/editor-egui-host/src/lib.rs` -> lines 722-731 show `let Some(rect) = self.viewport_tab_rect() else { return false; };`, defensive pixel-scale checks that also return `false`, and `rect.contains(logical)` only after those guards.
+- Claim: lifecycle tests currently cover no-host and no-cursor viewport hit-test behavior.
+  Falsifying read: `git grep -n -A8 -B2 "fn fresh_shell_reports_pointer_not_over_viewport\|fn shell_with_no_cursor_pos_reports_pointer_not_over_viewport" -- crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/tests.rs` -> lines 1115-1129 show both tests asserting `!shell.is_pointer_over_viewport_tab()`.
+- Claim: lifecycle tests currently cover zoom target, up vector, FOV, near/far clip, distance, and normalized direction preservation for the existing zoom helpers.
+  Falsifying read: `git grep -n -A40 -B4 "fn zoom_camera_in_and_out_preserve_target_and_direction" -- crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/tests.rs` -> lines 758-797 show the test capturing target/up/FOV/near/far/direction, calling `zoom_camera_in` and `zoom_camera_out`, and asserting those invariants.
+- Claim: the existing left-click route demonstrates viewport-tab fallthrough for face-pick using `is_pointer_over_viewport_tab`.
+  Falsifying read: `git grep -n -A26 -B2 "WindowEvent::MouseInput { state, button" -- crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs` -> lines 2538-2561 show the `MouseInput` branch computing `over_viewport = self.is_pointer_over_viewport_tab()` and passing it to `should_fire_face_pick`.
+
+## Constraints / Non-Goals
+
+- Do not edit `egui-host`, `editor-ui`, `editor-actions`, CAD crates, `kernel`, `runtime`, `editor/rge-editor`, Cargo metadata, workflows, automation scripts, or plugin-runtime paths.
+- Do not add new commands, accelerators, menu items, command-palette entries, route replacement, orbit navigation, pan navigation, drag navigation, hover behavior, clipboard behavior, CAD behavior, undo behavior, or dirty-state behavior.
+- Do not change the existing semantics of `zoom_camera_in`, `zoom_camera_out`, or `zoom_camera_by` except if a minimal private extraction is necessary and the tests prove behavior is unchanged.
+- Do not make mouse-wheel zoom global. It is viewport-only: the pointer must be over the host's Viewport tab body.
+- Do not require a real winit event loop, GPU surface, or egui-host edit to test the dispatch. Prefer a pure helper or private shell method that makes the routing decision testable inside `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/tests.rs`.
+
+## Verification Gates
+
+The Executor MUST run and document the result of each of these in their `EXECUTION_REPORT`:
+
+- `cargo test -p rge-editor-shell --lib` -> expected exit 0.
+- `cargo check -p rge-editor-shell --lib` -> expected exit 0.
+- `cargo fmt --check` -> expected exit 0.
+- `git diff --check` -> expected exit 0.
+- `git diff --name-only` -> expected changed files are limited to the MAY edit/MAY add surface plus generated dispatch artifacts.
+
+## Halt Conditions
+
+The Executor MUST halt without committing and request fresh guidance if any of the following occur:
+
+- Implementing viewport-only wheel zoom requires editing any forbidden path or adding any file outside MAY add new files.
+- The implementation appears to require changes to egui-host, editor-ui, editor-actions, commands, accelerators, menus, Cargo metadata, CAD/kernel/runtime/editor runtime code, workflows, automation, or plugin-runtime paths.
+- The implementation would change existing zoom semantics instead of reusing them.
+- The implementation would introduce orbit, pan, drag, route replacement, clipboard, CAD, undo, or dirty-state behavior.
+- Focused lifecycle tests cannot be written without a real window/event loop or without editing forbidden crates.
+- Any required verification gate fails and the fix would exceed this packet's scope.
+- Unexpected pre-existing dirty tracked files overlap the scoped files and the Executor cannot distinguish them from dispatch work.
+
+## Planner Notes
+
+Relevant current source anchors are grounded in the Current-State Claims above: `EditorShell::zoom_camera_in` and `zoom_camera_out` live in `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs` around the camera helper block; `is_pointer_over_viewport_tab` and the host viewport hit-test provide the existing no-host/no-cursor/no-viewport-rect false path; `WindowEvent::MouseInput` demonstrates viewport-tab fallthrough for face-pick; and existing lifecycle tests cover zoom target/direction preservation plus no-host/no-cursor viewport hit-test behavior. Keep the implementation as a small extension of those existing seams rather than creating a new input subsystem.
+
+For wheel sign, treat positive vertical deltas as zoom-in and negative vertical deltas as zoom-out. If both line and pixel deltas are supported, the sign of their vertical component should map identically. If the event includes only horizontal wheel movement, leave the camera unchanged.
